hadiths.ar:
1103  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ ن ْن كَّي ِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  إ َّن  َّهُّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ رَّج ُّل  عَّن ِ إَّلْق ِزَّإءَّة ِ ج َّلْف َّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامِ ق َّق َّال 
إ ِن  َّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامَّ ص َّامِن   لِلْق ِزَّإءَّة ِ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ ي َّص ْمَّن ُّ إَّلْأ ِمَّامُّ صَّلاَّة َّ إَّل َّذ ِي ن َّ هُّمْ مِن ْ ج َّلْق ِهِ إ ِن  َّمَّا ي َّص ْمَّن ُّ  إَّلْق ِزَّإءَّة َّ .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said:  
"Whoever leads a people in prayer while there is someone among them who is more 
knowledgeable than him, their affairs will continue to decline until the Day of 
Judgment."  
[AL SADUQ] 
Abu Dharr said:  
"Indeed, your Imam is your intercessor with Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic, so do 
not make your intercessor a foolish person or a transgressor."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1102
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٠٢

hadiths.ar:
1104  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال  لأَّ ي ُّصَّل ُّون َّ ن ِهِمْ 
صَّلاَّة ً ق َّزِي ص َّه ً ق ِى  ج َّمَّاعَّه ٍ إَّلْأ َّن ْزَّضُّ وَّ إَّلْمَّح ْذ ُّومُّ وَّ وَّلَّذُّ إَّلز ِّن َّا وَّ إَّلْأ َّعْزَّإن ِى  ُّ جَّي  َّى ي ُّهَّاج ِزَّ وَّ إَّلْمَّحْذُّوذُّ .
hadiths.en:
Al-Hussain ibn Kathir narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
that a man asked him about reciting (Qur'an) behind the Imam (during prayer).  
Imam {a.s} said: "No, the Imam is responsible for the recitation, but the Imam does not 
take responsibility for the prayers of those behind him; he is only responsible for the 
recitation." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 378 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 279 • Al -Istibsar, 
Part 1, p. 440 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1251 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 353 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 354 • Al -
Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 122 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 252 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1103
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٠٣

hadiths.ar:
1105  -  وَّق َّال إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
وَّلَّذِ إَّلز ِّن َّا وَّ إَّلْأ َّعْزَّإن ِى  ُّ لأَّ ي َّو ُّم ُّ إَّلْمُّهَّاج ِز .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} 
who said:  
"Five people should not lead others in prayer nor should they lead them in a 
congregational obligatory prayer: the person with leprosy (abrā ṣ), the person with a 
skin disease (majdhūm), the child of adultery (walad al-zina), the Bedouin (a‘rābī) until 
he migrates, and the person who has been subjected to the legal punishment (al -
maḥdūd)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 378 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1175 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 324 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1104
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٠٤

hadiths.ar:
1106   -  وَّق َّال عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ن  َّه ِ إ َّعْط َّمَّهَّا وَّ لأَّ ي ُّق ْن َّلُّ 
لَّهُّ س َّهَّاذَّة   وَّ لأَّ ي ُّصَّل َّى عَّلَّي ْهِ إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّكُّون َّ ي َّزَّك َّ ذ َّلِك َّ ج َّوْق اً عَّلَّى ن َّق ْسِهِ .
hadiths.en:
The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said:  
"None of you should pray behind a person with a mutilated limb (al-ajdham), a person 
with leprosy (al-abrāṣ), a person with a mental disorder (al-majnūn), a person who has 
been subjected to a legal punishment (al -maḥdūd), the child of adultery (walad al -
zina), or a Bedouin (al-a‘rābī) leading a migrant (al-muhājir)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 378 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 325 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1105
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٠٥

hadiths.en:
Imam {a.s} said: "An uncircumcised man (al-aghlaf) should not lead the people 
in prayer, even if he is the most proficient in reciting the Qur'an among them, because 
he has neglected one of the greatest aspects of the Sunnah. His testimony is not 
accepted, and prayers are not performed over him unless he left it (circumcision) out 
of fear for his life."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 378 • Ilal al-Sharai’ Vol. 2, p. 327 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, 
Part 3, p. 30 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1184 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 320 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 344 • Bihar 
Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 84 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 101, p. 112 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1106
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٠٦

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"A person in chains (al-qayd) does not lead those who are free, and a person afflicted 
with paralysis (al-falij) does not lead those who are healthy."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1107
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٠٧

hadiths.ar:
1109  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
إَّل َّي ِى  ق ِى  إَّلص ُّذُّورِ  »
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al -Baqir {a.s} and Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -
Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"There is no issue for a blind person to lead the prayer if the people (praying behind 
him) are satisfied with him and he is the best among them in recitation and the most 
knowledgeable in religious jurisprudence." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 379 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 338 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1108
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٠٨

hadiths.ar:
1110   -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ن ِالْق ِسْق ِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ مُّق ْي َّصِذإً .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"Indeed, the truly blind are those who are blind in their hearts, for it is not the eyes that 
go blind but the hearts within the chests." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 379 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 338 • Tafsir Nur Al -
Thaqalayn, Part 3, p. 508 • Tafseer Kanz Al-Daqaiq, Vol. 9, p. 117 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1109
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٠٩

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Three people should not be prayed behind: the one who is unknown (al -majhūl), the 
extremist (al -ghālī) even if he claims to hold your beliefs, and the one who openly 
commits sinful acts (al-mujāhir bil-fisq) even if he appears moderate."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 379 • Al-Khisal, Part 1, p. 154 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1181 
• Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 314 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 23 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 463 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1110
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١١٠

hadiths.ar:
1112   -  وَّ كَّي َّث َّ إ َّن ُّو عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلْن َّزْق ِى  ُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ إَّلي  َّان ِى  عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  إ َّ ي َّح ُّور ُّ ج ُّعِلْث ُّ ق ِذَّإك َّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ُّ ج َّلْف َّ مَّن ْ  
وَّق َّف َّ عَّلَّى إ َّن ِي ك َّ وَّ ج َّذِّك َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّا َّج َّاب .
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali ibn Muhammad al -Hadi {a.s} and Imam Muhammad ibn Ali al -Jawad 
{a.s} said:  
"Whoever believes in anthropomorphism (al-jism), do not give them anything from the 
zakat, and do not pray behind them." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 379 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 311 • Bihar Al Anwar, 
Vol. 85, p. 74 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1111
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١١١

hadiths.ar:
1113  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عُّمَّزُّ ن ْن ُّ ي َّز ِي ذَّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ إ ِمَّامٍ لأَّ ن َّا ْسَّ ن ِهِ ق ِى  ج َّمِي غِ إ ُّمُّورِةِ عَّارِف ٍ ع َّي ْزَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ي ُّسْمِغُّ 
إ َّن َّوَّي ْهِ إَّلْكَّلاَّمَّ إَّلْع َّلِي ظ َّ إَّل َّذ ِى  ي َّع ِي ط ُّهُّمَّا إ َّق ْزَّإ ُّ ج َّلْق َّهُّ ق َّال اً ق َّاطِعاً .
hadiths.en:
Abu Abdullah al-Barqi wrote to Abu Ja'far al-Thani {a.s}, asking:  
"May I be your ransom, is it permissible to pray behind someone who stopped at (only 
accepted the Imamate of) your father and grandfather {a.s}?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "Do not pray behind him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 379 • Awalim al-Ulum, Vol. 23, p. 402 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1112
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١١٢

hadiths.en:
Umar ibn Yazid asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} about an Imam 
who is generally good in all his affairs and knowledgeable, but he raises his voice and 
speaks harshly to his parents, causing them distress.  
He asked: "Should I pray behind him?"  
He {a.s} replied: "No, do not pray behind him as long as he is not openly defiant and 
severing ties”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 379 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 30 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, 
p. 1180 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 313 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 71, p. 44 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 40 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1113
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١١٣

hadiths.ar:
1115  -  وَّ رَّوَّى سَّعْذُّ ن ْن ُّ إ ِسْمَّاعِي لَّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِي هِ عَّن ِ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّالَّ: سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي ُّق َّارِف ُّ إَّلذ  َّن ْث َّ ي ُّصَّل َّى  
ج َّلْق َّهُّ إ َّمْ لأَّ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Halabi narrated from him {a.s} that He said:  
"Do not pray behind someone who declares you to be an unbeliever (kafir), and do not 
pray behind someone whom you have declared to be an unbeliever."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 380 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1182 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 311 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1114
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١١٤

hadiths.en:
Saʿd ibn Ismail narrated from his father, who narrated from al-Ridha {a.s}, that 
He said:  
"I asked him about a man who commits sins ; should one pray behind him or not?"  
Imam {a.s} said: "No." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 380 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 40 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1115
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١١٥

hadiths.ar:
1117 - وَّق َّال إ ِسْمَّاعِي لُّ إَّلْح ُّعْق ِى  ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : رَّج ُّل  ي ُّحِث  ُّ إ َّمِي زَّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ لأَّ ي َّي َّن َّز َّإ ُّ مِن ْ 
عَّذُّوِّةِ وَّ ي َّق ُّولُّ هُّوَّ إ َّجَّث  ُّ إ ِلَّى  َّ مِم َّن ْ ج َّالَّق َّهُّ ق َّال لأَّ كَّزَّإمَّه َّ إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّي  َّق ِي َّهُّ .  
وق ال إ ن ى  رص ى  إلله عن ه ق ى  رسالي ه إ لى : لأ ي صل ج لف  إ جذ إ لأ ج لف  رج لي ن  إ جذهما من  ي ي ق  ن ذي ن ه وورعه، وإ ج ز ي ي ق ى   
سي ق ه وسطوي ه وس ن اعي ه على إلذي ن ، وصل ج لق ه على سن ي ل إلي ق ي ه  وإلمذإرإة  وإ ذ ن  لن ق سك  وإ ق م وإق زإ  لها ع ي ز مو ي م ن ه ق ا ن  
ق زع ث  من  ق زإءة  إلسورة  ق ن له ق ا ن ق    من ها إ ي ه  ومح ذ إلله عز  وج ل، ق ا ذ إ ركغ إلأمام ق اق زء إلأ ي ه  وإركغ ن ها، ق ا ن  لم ي لحق  إلق زإءة  
وج س ي ث  إ ن  ي زكغ ق ق ل ما جذ ق ه إلأمام من  إلأ ذ إن  وإلأ ق امه  وإركغ، وإ ن  كن ث  ق ى  صلاة  ن اق له  وإ ق ي مث  إلصلاة  ق اق طعها وصل 
إلق زي ص ه ، وإ ن  كن ث  ق ى  إلق زي ص ه  ق لا ي ق طعها وإج علها ن اق له  وسلم ق ى  إل زكعي ي ن ، ي م صل  مغ إلأمام إ لأ إ ن  ي كون  إلأمام ممن  
ي ي ق ى ق لا ي ق طغ صلاي ك  ولأ ي ح علها ن اق له  ولكن  إ ج ظ إ لى إلصف  وصل معه، ق ا ذ إ ق ام إلأمام إ لى رإن عي ه ق ق م معه وي س هذ من  
ق ي ام وسلم من  ق ي ام .
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Isma'il ibn Muslim that he asked Imam Jafar ibn 
Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about praying behind a man who denies (or rejects) the 
decree (Qadar) of Allah {SWT}, the Mighty and Majestic.  
He (Al-Sadiq) replied: "He should repeat every prayer he performed behind him”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 380 • Monotheism, Part 1, p. 383 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 
1182 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 311 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 375 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 85, p. 73 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1116
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١١٦

hadiths.ar: 1118   -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  إ ِن  َّ رَّسُّولَّ إَّلل َّهِ صَّل َّى إَّلل َّهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ وَّ إ لِهِ صَّل َّى ن ِا َّصْحَّان ِهِ ج َّالِساً ق َّلَّم َّا ق َّزَّع َّ ق َّالَّ
hadiths.en:
Isma'il al-Ju'fi said to Abu Ja'far:  
"A man loves The Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} but does not 
dissociate from his enemies, and he says,  
'He (Ali) is dearer to me than those who opposed him.'"  
He (Abu Ja'far) replied: "This person is of mixed (belief) and is an enemy; do not pray 
behind him, nor is there any respect (to be shown to him) except if you must do so out 
of precaution." 
[AL SADUQ] 
My father (may Allah {SWT} be pleased with him) said in his letter to me:  
"Do not pray behind anyone except for one of two types of people: either someone 
whose religion and piety you trust, or someone you must pray behind out of fear of 
their power, sword, or harshness towards the religion. Pray behind them as a form of 
precaution and accommodation, and in this case, you may make your own intention 
and recite for yourself without following them as an Imam. If you finish reciting a surah 
before them, leave one verse and continue praising Allah {SWT} until the Imam bows, at 
which point you should finish the verse and bow. If you are performing a 
supererogatory (nafl) prayer and the congregational prayer is established, stop and 
join the obligatory prayer. If you are in an obligatory prayer, do not stop it; instead, 
make it a supere rogatory prayer, complete it with two units, and then join the Imam. 
However, if the Imam is one you fear, do not interrupt your prayer or make it a 
supererogatory prayer; simply continue, move to the row, and pray alongside the 
congregation. When the Imam  stands for the fourth unit, stand with him, recite the 
tashahhud from a standing position, and conclude the prayer."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
« .

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1117
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١١٧

hadiths.ar:
1119  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  «
ن ِهِمْ ج َّالِساً ق ِى  ع ُّزْق َّه ِ إ ُّمِّ إ ِن ْزَّإهِي م .
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
"The Messenger of Allah {saws} once led his companions in prayer while seated. When 
he finished, He {saws} said: 'None of you should lead others in prayer while seated after 
me.'". 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 381 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1263 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 345 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1118
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١١٨

hadiths.ar:
1120 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّ ج َّمِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ صَّالِحٍ : إ َّي  ُّهُّمَّا إ َّق ْص َّلُّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ لِن َّق ْسِهِ ق ِى  إ َّو َّلِ إَّلْوَّق ْث ِ إ َّوْ ي ُّو َّج ِّزُّ ق َّلِي لاً وَّ ي ُّصَّلِّى  ن ِا َّهْلِ  
مَّسْح ِذِةِ إ ِذ َّإ كَّان َّ إ ِمَّامَّهُّمْ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"The Prophet {saws} once fell from a horse, injuring the right side of his body, and he led 
the people in prayer while seated in the room of Umm Ibrahim”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1119

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١١٩

hadiths.ar:
1121   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ رَّج ُّل  ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهُّ إ ِن  َّ لِى  مَّسْح ِذإً عَّلَّى ن َّاب ِ ذَّإرِى  ق َّا َّي  ُّهُّمَّا إ َّق ْص َّلُّ إ ُّصَّلِّى  ق ِى  مَّن ْز ِلِى  ق َّا ُّطِي ل   إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ إ َّوْ 
إ ُّصَّلِّى  ن ِهِمْ وَّ إ ُّج َّق ِّف ُّ ق َّكَّي َّث َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم .
hadiths.en:
Jamil ibn Salih asked:  
"Which is better, for a man to pray by himself at the beginning of the time (for prayer) 
or to delay a little and pray with the people of his mosque if he is their Imam?"  
He {a.s} replied: "He should delay and pray with the people of his mosque if he is their 
Imam." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 381 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 429 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1120
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٢٠

hadiths.ar:
1122  -  وَّ إ ِن  َّ عَّلِي  اً عَّ ق َّالَّ: ق ِى  رَّج ُّلَّي ْن ِ إِج ْي َّلَّق َّا ق َّق َّالَّ إ َّجَّذُّهُّمَّا كُّن ْث ُّ إ ِمَّامَّك وَّق َّالَّ  إَّلْأ ج َّزُّ كُّن ْث ُّ إ ِمَّامَّك َّ ق َّال 
ي َّام َّهوَّق َّالَّ إَّلْأ ج َّزُّ كُّن ْث ُّ إ َّن ْي َّم ُّ ن ِك َّ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
A man asked him:  
"I have a mosque at the entrance of my house. Which is better - should I pray at home 
where I can make my prayer longer, or should I lead them (in the mosque) and make it 
lighter?"  
He {a.s} wrote: "Lead them in prayer and perform it well, but do not make it too heavy 
(burdensome)”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, V. 1, p. 381 • Al-Wafi, V. 8, p. 1171 • Wasail Al-Shia, V. 8, p. 430 
﴿HADITH ﴾
